methodology_note: Supply events are derived from ERC-20 Transfer logs. Mint means Transfer from zero address. Burn-to-zero means Transfer to zero address. Transfers to 0xdead are reported separately because they usually do not reduce ERC-20 totalSupply.

chain_summaries.name: Cellframe ERC20 v2
chain_summaries.note: event_implied_supply uses mints minus burns to zero address. Dead-address transfers are reported separately because not every ERC-20 reduces totalSupply when tokens are sent to 0xdead.

important_caveats: Event-implied supply should approximately match contract totalSupply when all logs from deployment are scanned.
If start_block is too late, historical mint/burn events will be missed.
Transfers to 0xdead may be economically burned but usually do not reduce totalSupply.
Raw ETH + BSC totalSupply can double count bridged supply if one side is locked while another side is minted.
This scan explains supply-changing events. It does not quantify route exposure or final sale proceeds.
